page: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server built on the x86_64 architecture and managed via the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor configured with 12 vCPUs across 12 physical cores, running at a clock speed of 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation. The system is equipped with 128.0 GB of system memory, providing 10.67 GB of RAM per core. Graphics and parallel computing acceleration are delivered via a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 48 GB of dedicated VRAM. Local storage is not bundled with this instance type, and it includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Benchmark data reveals top-tier single-core performance, with the stress-ng single-core test and Geekbench single-core score both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Single-threaded tasks such as gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also achieve top-tier throughput. Multi-core performance, including Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory performance is strong, with large-block read and write bandwidths ranking in the top 25%. In LLM inference benchmarks, the server demonstrates top-tier text generation speeds for small models and strong generation speeds for medium-sized models, while large model inference speeds remain average.

From a resource density perspective, this server offers a qualitative balance by pairing a high-end NVIDIA L40S GPU and a substantial 128 GB memory footprint with a shared CPU allocation. This configuration is highly efficient for workloads that require heavy GPU acceleration and high single-core CPU throughput but do not demand fully dedicated multi-core CPU resources. It is well-suited for machine learning inference, image processing, data science tasks, and text processing workloads.
description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server utilizing the x86_64 architecture. It features 12 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with a shared CPU allocation, 128.0 GB of system memory, and a single NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. Local storage is not included. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, alongside strong memory bandwidth for large blocks. The server excels in LLM inference for small and medium models, delivering top-tier and strong text generation speeds respectively. Multi-core CPU performance and database operations remain in the average tier. This hardware profile is optimized for workloads such as machine learning inference, image processing, and text processing, offering a qualitative balance of high-capacity GPU resources and system memory without the cost of fully dedicated multi-core CPU allocations.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated server featuring an AMD EPYC 9575F CPU, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM, optimized for machine learning and image processing.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-12xCPU-128GB-1xL40S features 12 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU, delivering top-tier single-core and LLM inference performance.
tagline: A GPU-accelerated server combining top-tier single-core performance and 48 GB of VRAM for efficient machine learning inference and data processing.
bullet_points: NVIDIA Ada Lovelace L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for parallel computing.
AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 12 vCPUs and 128 GB of system memory.
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance.
Strong memory bandwidth and efficient small-to-medium LLM inference.
Shared CPU allocation with no local storage bundled.
Optimized for machine learning inference, image processing, and text processing.
